title: APC PDU SNMP Bank Load Versus Breaker Headroom
dataSources: `index=power|cooling|environment` · `sourcetype="snmp:apc:pdu"` (APC PowerNet / AirIR / ATS / NetBotz OIDs per deployment)
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (SC4SNMP) or SNMP Modular Input; APC PowerNet-MIB / AirIR-MIB field extractions
spl:
index=power sourcetype="snmp:apc:pdu" earliest=-1h
| eval bank_a=tonumber(coalesce(rPDULoadStatusLoad, phase_load_a, ""))/10
| eval bank_b=tonumber(coalesce(rPDULoadStatusLoad2, phase_load_b, ""))/10
| eval headroom=32-bank_a
| where headroom < 4 OR bank_b>28
| table pdu_name bank_a bank_b headroom
description: Approaching breaker limits risks nuisance trips during inrush when new gear powers on.
value: Prevents cascading outages during maintenance windows.
implementation: (1) Map breaker rating per feed. (2) Use actual nameplate not nominal. (3) Pair with change freeze rules.
grandmaExplanation: We compare rack power strips to their limits so breakers do not trip unexpectedly.
